page: Amazon Web Services t3a.micro is a burstable virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 1 physical core hyper-threaded to provide 2 vCPUs, supported by 1.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks place the t3a.micro in the lower tiers of compute and memory throughput. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, along with PassMark CPU scores, rank in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations on large blocks, also fall within the bottom 10% category. However, the CPU supports hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI flags, yielding a slightly higher relative score in the PassMark CPU encryption test, which ranks in the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the t3a.micro offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for workloads that do not require sustained high CPU utilization. Because of its burstable CPU allocation and low baseline network bandwidth, it is best suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, development environments, and basic background tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services t3a.micro is a burstable, budget-friendly instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7571 processor clocked at 2.2 GHz. It provides 2 vCPUs via 1 physical core with hyper-threading, paired with 1.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks, including stress-ng and memory bandwidth tests, consistently rank in the bottom 10%, though hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES and SHA_NI) offers slightly better relative performance in encryption tests. Given its burstable CPU allocation and low resource footprint, the t3a.micro is qualitatively highly cost-efficient for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, code repositories, and development or testing environments that require intermittent compute bursts rather than sustained high performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.micro is a burstable virtual instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, 2 vCPUs, and 1.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, designed for low-intensity workloads requiring intermittent compute bursts.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3a.micro is a burstable x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 1.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for budget-friendly, low-duty tasks.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable x86_64 instance with AMD EPYC processing and minimal memory footprint for light, intermittent workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.2 GHz AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 2 vCPUs
Equipped with 1.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
Burstable CPU allocation ideal for intermittent workloads
Features hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for low-traffic web serving and development environments
